extra.title_en: TITLE: Reuters: Кремль готував схему з перевезенням 100 тисяч виборців для впливу на вибори у Вірменії | LEDE: Російські посадовці обговорювали можливість перевезення до 100 тисяч вірмен, які проживають у РФ, для участі в парламентських виборах у Вірменії та голосування проти чинного прем'єра Нікола Пашиняна.
extra.title_original: Reuters: Кремль готував схему з перевезенням 100 тисяч виборців для впливу на вибори у Вірменії

original_text: [TITLE: Reuters: Кремль готував схему з перевезенням 100 тисяч виборців для впливу на вибори у Вірменії | LEDE: Російські посадовці обговорювали можливість перевезення до 100 тисяч вірмен, які проживаю] (uk: Reuters: Кремль готував схему з перевезенням 100 тисяч виборців для впливу на ви) — Російські посадовці обговорювали можливість перевезення до 100 тисяч вірмен, які проживають у РФ, для участі в парламентських виборах у Вірменії та голосування проти чинного прем'єра Нікола Пашиняна.

extra.title_original: Президент Румунії скликав Раду нацбезпеки через влучання дрона у Галаці, в МЗС викликали посла РФ

original_text: [TITLE: Президент Румунії скликав Раду нацбезпеки через влучання дрона у Галаці, в МЗС викликали посла РФ | LEDE: Очільниця МЗС Румунії Оана Цою повідомила в п'ятницю, що викликала посла Російської Фед] (uk: Президент Румунії скликав Раду нацбезпеки через влучання дрона у Галаці, в МЗС в) — Очільниця МЗС Румунії Оана Цою повідомила в п'ятницю, що викликала посла Російської Федерації, щоб повідомити йому про заходи, яких вживатиме Румунія на дипломатичному рівні після того, як російський безпілот

original_text: [TITLE: Росія атакувала Україну "Іскандером" та 323 БпЛА, 217 дронів знешкодили | LEDE: Російські війська вночі атакували Україну ракетою і 232 дронами, ППО знешкодила 217 дронів.] (uk: Росія атакувала Україну "Іскандером" та 323 БпЛА, 217 дронів знешкодили) — Російські війська вночі атакували Україну ракетою і 232 дронами, ППО знешкодила 217 дронів.

extra.title_en: TITLE: У російському Ярославлі після атаки дронів горять "об'єкти для зберігання пального" | LEDE: У Росії заявили про атаку безпілотників і перекрили трасу з Ярославля на Москву.
extra.title_original: У російському Ярославлі після атаки дронів горять "об'єкти для зберігання пального"

original_text: [TITLE: У російському Ярославлі після атаки дронів горять "об'єкти для зберігання пального" | LEDE: У Росії заявили про атаку безпілотників і перекрили трасу з Ярославля на Москву.] (uk: У російському Ярославлі після атаки дронів горять "об'єкти для зберігання пально) — У Росії заявили про атаку безпілотників і перекрили трасу з Ярославля на Москву.

extra.title_en: TITLE: У ще одному районі Румунії знайшли великий дрон | LEDE: На північному заході Румунії, у повіті Марамуреш, було виявлено дрон без вибухового навантаження.
extra.title_original: У ще одному районі Румунії знайшли великий дрон

original_text: [TITLE: У ще одному районі Румунії знайшли великий дрон | LEDE: На північному заході Румунії, у повіті Марамуреш, було виявлено дрон без вибухового навантаження.] (uk: У ще одному районі Румунії знайшли великий дрон) — На північному заході Румунії, у повіті Марамуреш, було виявлено дрон без вибухового навантаження.

extra.title_en: TITLE: Минулої доби ворог атакував 267 разів, скинув 269 керованих авіабомб – Генштаб | LEDE: За добу 28 травня Генеральний штаб зафіксував 267 бойових зіткнень на фронті.

original_text: [TITLE: Минулої доби ворог атакував 267 разів, скинув 269 керованих авіабомб – Генштаб | LEDE: За добу 28 травня Генеральний штаб зафіксував 267 бойових зіткнень на фронті.] (uk: Минулої доби ворог атакував 267 разів, скинув 269 керованих авіабомб – Генштаб) — За добу 28 травня Генеральний штаб зафіксував 267 бойових зіткнень на фронті.

Ukraine Offers Romania Drone Defense Cooperation After Russian Drone Hits Apartment Block — Ukraine is ready to deepen cooperation with Romania to counter Russian drone threats after a Russian drone struck a residential building in Galați on Friday. Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiha said the incident highlights the broader risk Russia’s war poses to the Black Sea region and Europe, while NATO and EU officials condemned it as a dangerous escalation.

Ukraine Claims Crimea–Donbas Land Corridor Effectively Cut As Logistics Routes Come Under Fire — Ukraine’s military intelligence (HUR) on Friday said it is disrupting Russian logistics in occupied southern Ukraine by targeting the land corridor linking Crimea with Donbas. Officials claim key routes between Berdyansk, Melitopol, and Dzhankoi are under fire control, with strikes destroying fuel and transport vehicles and worsening Russia’s supply and infrastructure capacity in the occupied territo

Russia’s War Costs Could Exceed Budget by $28 Billion — Russia’s war spending could surpass planned budget levels by 2 trillion rubles ($28 billion) this year, according to a report citing a letter from Finance Minister Anton Siluanov. The document reportedly warned of growing fiscal pressures and urged spending freezes in other sectors to offset rising military costs. The report highlights mounting strains on Russia’s finances due to record defense spending.

Volgograd Refinery, Yaroslavl Fuel Sites Hit as Moscow Claims 208 Ukrainian Drones Downed — Ukraine reportedly launched a large-scale overnight drone assault targeting Russian oil and fuel infrastructure across multiple regions, including Volgograd, Yaroslavl and Krasnodar Krai. Russian officials reported strikes on refineries, fuel storage sites and the Temryuk seaport, while Russia’s defense ministry claimed to have downed 208 drones.

‘Crossed Yet Another Line’: EU And NATO React After Russian Drone Hits Romania Home — NATO, the EU, and regional leaders condemned a Russian drone incident in which a residential building in Romania’s Galati region was struck Friday morning, injuring two people. Officials called it a dangerous escalation and a threat to NATO security. Leaders including Ursula von der Leyen and Maia Sandu pledged solidarity and warned Russia had crossed a new line.

Russian Drone Strike Hits Cargo Ship En Route From Odesa Region To Turkey, Ukraine Says — A cargo ship ANT sailing from Ukraine’s Odesa region to Turkey was struck Friday morning by a drone in the Black Sea, causing a fire and injuring two crew members, Ukraine’s Navy said. The vessel was later evacuated and the blaze extinguished. Kyiv attributed the attack to Russian forces, warning of continued risks to civilian shipping.

War Trauma Pushes More Ukrainians to Become Artists — Ukraine’s war has pushed many artists to abandon conventional careers and turn to art as a way to process trauma, loss, and displacement. Featured at Kyiv Art Fair, artists including Olena Kharakhulakh and Irina Cheremisina say creativity became essential for survival, healing, and preserving their connection to Ukraine amid Russia’s invasion.

US Congressman Says Lavrov’s Threats Show Russia ‘Desperate’ as Kyiv Defies Evacuation Calls — US Congressional Representative Jim Himes said Kremlin Foreign Minister Lavrov’s threats against diplomats in Kyiv show Russia is “desperate” on the battlefield. Despite Moscow’s warnings of further attacks, foreign diplomats refused to leave the capital as US lawmakers pledged continued support and more military aid for Ukraine.
ISW Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, May 28, 2026 — Latest from the Institute for the Study of War.

Ukraine’s Envoy Melnyk Blasts Russia’s ‘Fake’ Starobilsk Narrative at UN — Ukraine’s UN envoy Andriy Melnyk accused Russia of inventing a false victimhood narrative over the alleged Starobilsk dormitory strike, saying Moscow’s own published list showed no children among the named victims. He also condemned Russia’s massive attack on Kyiv, warned Belarus against deeper involvement in the war, and urged allies to increase air-defense support tenfold.

Russian Drone Hits Apartment Building in NATO Member Romania, Injuring Two — Romania’s defense ministry said a Russian drone entered Romanian airspace during Moscow’s overnight attacks on Ukraine and crashed onto the roof of an apartment building in Galați. The impact triggered a fire, and emergency services said two people were wounded.

Kyiv Briefly Under Air Alert Over Ballistic Missile Threat — Kyiv and several Ukrainian regions were briefly placed under air raid alerts in the early hours of Friday after the Air Force warned of a ballistic missile threat to eastern, northern and central regions. The warning came amid repeated Russian missile threats, including from MiG-31K aircraft capable of carrying Kinzhal ballistic missiles.

Ukraine Issues Nationwide Missile Warning Over MiG-31K Takeoff — Ukraine issued a nationwide missile threat warning early Friday after detecting the takeoff of a Russian MiG-31K, a carrier of the Kinzhal ballistic missile. Air raid alerts were activated across all regions, and residents were urged to take shelter.

UN Condemns Escalation in Ukraine War After Russian Strikes — UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres and UN rights chief Volker Turk warned that Russia’s intensifying strikes on Ukraine risk pushing the war into a dangerous new phase. The UN rights office recorded a 21 percent rise in civilian casualties in the first four months of 2026.

Washington’s Step Back From Ukraine Mediation May Help Kyiv, Former US Ambassador Says — Former US Ambassador to Ukraine Steven Pifer argues that Washington’s Russia-Ukraine mediation effort has failed because the Trump administration pressured Kyiv while avoiding real pressure on Moscow. Pifer said a pause in US mediation could spare Ukraine from one-sided concessions and create space for a more balanced European role.

Ukraine Fortifies Belarus Border Amid Growing Threats — Ukraine is strengthening its entire border with Belarus, with new engineering works along the frontier and deeper fortifications behind it. Officials say the whole 1,085-kilometer line is already protected, but construction is expanding as Kyiv prepares for possible Russian escalation from the north.

Poland’s Orlen Energy Group Says It Is ‘in Talks’ Over a Stake in Ukraine’s Ukrnafta — Poland’s largest energy concern Orlen has entered talks over acquiring a stake in Ukrnafta, CEO Ireneusz Fafara said Thursday, describing the company as one of several partners in an active negotiation process. The move follows years of deepening commercial ties: Orlen’s Mažeikiai refinery sells nearly 18 percent of its output in Ukraine, and the group ships around 1.5 million tons of fuel products there annua

Latvia Installs ‘Dragon Teeth’ on Russian Border as NATO Assigns New Corps to Defend Baltics — Latvia has begun installing concrete “dragon’s teeth” anti-tank barriers on its border with Russia, as a part of the Baltic Defense Line agreed by Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania. At the same time, NATO is preparing to assign the German-Netherlands Corps, based in Münster, splitting responsibility on the eastern flank with the existing Multinational Corps Northeast in Szczecin.

‘Oreshnik’ Use Backfires On Putin By Exposing Russian Missile Tech – US Lawmaker — A US lawmaker said Russia’s use of the “Oreshnik” missile in Ukraine may backfire by giving Kyiv and Western analysts access to debris that reveals its capabilities. Jim Himes said the strikes fail to intimidate Ukrainians and instead provide intelligence gains, while questions persist over the missile’s composition and effectiveness.

US, Iran Agree Deal Framework, Awaiting Trump Approval: US Sources — US and Iranian negotiators agreed on a framework to extend a 60-day ceasefire and launch nuclear talks, pending Donald Trump’s approval. The deal would ease shipping restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz and address uranium stockpiles, though tensions and mutual truce violations persist.

Europe Cannot Be Both Ukraine’s Ally and Russia’s Negotiating Partner – Norway’s Foreign Minister — Norwegian Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide said Europe should not act as a mediator in Russia-Ukraine peace talks and should instead increase military support for Ukraine. Estonian and EU officials echoed this view, arguing neutrality would weaken Europe’s leverage and could ultimately advantage the Kremlin

Latvia Gets New Centre-Right Govt After Row Over Stray Ukraine Drones — Latvia’s parliament approved a new centre-right coalition government led by Andris Kulbergs after the previous cabinet collapsed over a dispute involving stray Ukrainian drones and air defence failures. The new government pledged stronger security, continued support for Ukraine, and a firm pro-EU, pro-NATO foreign policy ahead of October elections.

Drones Strike 3 Russian ‘Shadow Fleet’ Tankers in Black Sea Near Turkey — Three Russia-linked “shadow fleet” tankers were reportedly attacked by drones in the Black Sea near Turkey, with no casualties. Ukraine’s HUR says the vessels were used to evade sanctions.

Ukraine Ready to Pay for More Patriot Systems, Stefanishyna Says — Kyiv is prepared to pay for additional Patriot air defense systems and missiles as Russia escalates attacks against Ukrainian cities, Ukraine’s Ambassador to the US Olha Stefanishyna said on Thursday after Zelensky sent an urgent letter to Trump and the US Congress warning of critical shortages. Kyiv says ballistic missile defense remains almost entirely dependent on the US.

EU Approves €2.8B Ukraine Facility Payment — The EU Council on May 28 approved a €2.8 billion ($3.25 billion) disbursement to Ukraine under the Ukraine Facility after Kyiv completed 11 of 20 required reform steps. Ukraine has now fulfilled 86 reform benchmarks in total, with another 65 underway, and will for the first time receive compensation for reforms completed ahead of schedule.

Resilience Within Music — After Russia’s full-scale invasion scattered her choir across Europe, a pianist in Kharkiv rebuilt a community where singing together offers a brief distance from the war surrounding the city.

Putin Travels Through Kazakhstan Under Heavy Security — Russian leader Vladimir Putin on Thursday traveled through Kazakhstan’s capital under heavy security measures that included armored vehicles, motorcycles, military personnel and aerial surveillance, according to footage.

Belarusian Components Found in Russian ‘Oreshnik’ Missile — Ukrainian officials showed European ambassadors foreign-made components extracted from Russian missiles and drones used in attacks against Ukraine, including parts from Belarusian manufacturers found in the “Oreshnik” missile. Kyiv urged Europe to tighten sanctions enforcement against Russia and Belarus, warning that Moscow continues relying on foreign technologies and shadow fleet revenues to sustain its war effort.

Russia Deploys New Air Defense Systems on Moscow Rooftops — Russia is reinforcing Moscow’s air defenses by installing Pantsir-SMD-E systems on rooftops across the city, including civilian buildings and business centers, according to analysts and media reports published Thursday.

Kallas Says Limits Demanded From Ukraine Must Be ‘Mirrored’ to Russia — EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Russia should face the same military restrictions it demands from Ukraine in any future peace agreement. Speaking on Thursday, Kallas argued that Moscow’s conditions remain “maximalist” and said European security concerns also include Russian troops stationed in countries such as Georgia and Moldova.

Sweden Hands Ukraine 16 Gripen Jets Armed With 200-km Missiles, Opens Deal for 22 More — Sweden announced it will transfer 16 Gripen C/D fighter jets to Ukraine free of charge and plans to sell 22 additional Gripen E aircraft. The agreement was unveiled during a joint briefing by Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson and President Volodymyr Zelensky at Uppsala Air Base. Kyiv says the jets, equipped with long-range missiles, will help push Russian aircraft farther from Ukrainian territory and strengthen

Congress Finally Ready to Vote to Help Ukraine, Punish Russia — Congress has the power to act now to help Ukraine and punish Russia when the Trump Administration will not. The US House of Representatives is finally ready to vote the first week in June on Russian sanctions legislation to help Ukraine and punish Russia for its illegal aggression and ongoing war crimes against the people of Ukraine.

Putin, Tokayev Sign ‘7 Principles of Friendship’ — Russian President Vladimir Putin and Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev signed a joint declaration outlining seven principles of friendship and cooperation between Russia and Kazakhstan during Putin’s state visit to Astana. The document emphasizes shared history, economic partnership, Eurasian integration and cultural ties.

Trump Threatened or Attacked 1 in 13 Countries, CNN Says — US President Donald Trump has threatened or used force against at least 15 countries during his time in office, according to a CNN analysis published on Wednesday, after Trump warned Oman it could be “blown up” over tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

‘One-Way Ticket’: Nigerians Promised Russian Jobs, Sent by Kremlin to Die in Ukraine — Ukrainian military intelligence said Nigerian national Ayebusiwa Olabode Victor was killed while fighting for Russia in Ukraine’s Kharkiv region after allegedly being lured with promises of employment. HUR said the case reflects a broader Russian recruitment scheme targeting African citizens, who allegedly arrive in Russia expecting jobs but end up forced into military contracts and deployed to the front line.

Ukraine Allocates $244M for Weapons Production, Defense Expansion — The Ukrainian government approved an additional Hr.10.8 billion ($244 million) in defense funding for weapons procurement and domestic arms production, Most of the funds will go toward purchasing, repairing and modernizing military equipment, while nearly Hr.2 billion will support expansion of Ukraine’s defense industry and battlespace technologies.

US Embassy Refutes Claims of Exit From Kyiv — The US Embassy in Kyiv denied reports that it had evacuated the Ukrainian capital following Russian threats of new missile strikes. Thursday’s statement came after EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas suggested the American embassy had left the city while European diplomatic missions remained. Ukrainian officials also rejected the claims, saying the embassy continues operating normally in Kyiv.

Rada Ratifies Zelensky’s €90B EU Loan for Ukraine’s Defense and Budget Support — The Verkhovna Rada has ratified a bill submitted by President Zelensky approving an EU loan package of up to €90 billion ($104 billion) for Ukraine. The funding, planned for 2026–2027, will support both defense spending and state budget stability. The agreement includes concessional terms, with interest covered by the EU and repayment of principal linked to potential reparations from Russia.

Kremlin Deepens Taliban Partnership With New Military-Technical Agreement — Russia and the Taliban signed a military-technical cooperation agreement during a security forum in the Moscow region, highlighting Moscow’s steadily expanding ties with Kabul. The contents of the agreement have not been officially disclosed. The move follows Russia’s suspension of the Taliban’s terrorist designation in 2025.

Dragon Capital Closes Ukraine's First Infrastructure Fund at $240 Million; EBRD, IFC, and EIB Anchor the Round — Dragon Capital and Amber Infrastructure have completed the first close of the Amber Dragon Ukraine Infrastructure Fund I, raising €207 million ($240 million) from anchor investors including the EBRD, EIB, and IFC. The fund will target energy, transport, and digital sectors, with first deals expected imminently. The company called it the first dedicated infrastructure vehicle in Ukrain
ISW Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, May 27, 2026 — Latest from the Institute for the Study of War.

Zelensky Sounds Alarm on New Russian Terror Wave, Urges Immediate Patriot Shipments — In a letter to Trump and Congress, president Zelensky on Wednesday said Ukraine has made major gains against Russian drones but urgently needs US anti-ballistic missiles.

Unified Front: Zelensky, Tsikhanouskaya Address Russian Threats and Lukashenko’s War Role — Belarusian democratic leader Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya met President Volodymyr Zelensky in Kyiv during her first working visit to Ukraine, marking a major step in formalizing ties between Kyiv and Belarusian democratic forces. The meeting focused on countering Russian threats, isolating Alexander Lukashenko’s regime, border security, Belarusian volunteers fighting for Ukraine, political prisoners and accou

Trump Endorses Pashinyan Ahead of Crucial Armenia Vote — US President Donald Trump has endorsed Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan ahead of a key parliamentary election, praising his peace agenda and plans for the US-backed “Trump Route” corridor. The endorsement came a day after Secretary of State Marco Rubio visited Yerevan, where Washington and Armenia signed agreements on strategic partnership, critical minerals and regional connectivity.

Ukraine to Be Fully Integrated Into EU Air Defense, Drone Priorities, Von der Leyen Says — European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said Ukraine will be included in Europe’s urgent defense efforts, backed by a €28.3 billion ($32.9 billion) loan for Kyiv’s military needs.

Sybiha Calls for Airport or Seaport Truce as First Step Toward Peace — Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Europe should first push for a limited airport or seaport truce, using sanctions and frozen Russian assets to press Moscow toward practical peace steps.

Northolt Treaty Deepens UK-Poland Defense Ties Amid Fears for NATO’s Future — UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer and Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk have signed the Northolt Treaty, a defense and security pact that will strengthen air defense cooperation and joint efforts against Russian hybrid threats, as well as joint action plan on irregular migration. The deal comes amid reports of Russia stepping up troop mobilization, with recent drone and missile incidents concerning NATO’s eastern flan

Trump’s Gaza Peace Board Raises Zero Dollars, Reports Say — Trump’s Board of Peace for Gaza has raised no money into its official World Bank fund four months after launch and multibillion-dollar pledges, the Financial Times reported. Instead, donors have sent limited contributions to a private JPMorgan account that is not subject to the same independent oversight, with none of the promised US support being deployed for rebuilding on the ground yet.

Ukraine Ready to Buy Patriots as Zelensky Warns Russia Prepares New Wave of ‘Terror Strikes’ — President Zelensky appealed to US President Trump and Congress for expanded air defense support, warning that Russia is escalating missile and drone attacks on Ukraine. He said a recent strike included hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles and stressed that Ukraine remains heavily dependent on US Patriot systems to counter ballistic threats. Zelensky also called for deeper defense cooperation and w

Zelensky Says Russia’s Mobilization Push Is Proof Moscow ‘Not Preparing for Real Diplomacy’ — President Zelensky said that Ukraine has growing intelligence indicating Russia is preparing for an additional mobilization wave to compensate for high casualties, with Moscow’s alleged order to increase its forces by tens of thousands. Zelensky called the move proof that Russia is not preparing for real diplomacy and said Kyiv could share the data with partners.

Kharkiv Opens First Underground Kindergarten Amid Intensifying Russian Strikes — Kharkiv Mayor Ihor Terekhov says the city has been compelled to build 10 underground schools and is currently constructing its first underground kindergarten. Speaking at the GLOBSEC Forum, he said Russian attacks on civilian infrastructure have reshaped children’s education in Kharkiv, and called on the international community to continue supporting Ukraine.

Explainer: Russia’s Grip on Its Neighbors Is Loosening — Russia’s war on Ukraine has reshaped Moscow’s relationships across its former sphere of influence. Kazakhstan and Azerbaijan have quietly distanced themselves while preserving pragmatic ties with Russia. Armenia has made the most dramatic pivot, freezing CSTO membership and deepening EU and US partnerships. Moldova is pushing ahead with EU integration and Transnistria reintegration. Georgia remains an outlier, with its government drifting

Zelensky Warns Trump of Critical Air Defense Shortage — President Zelensky sent an urgent letter to US President Trump and Congress warning that Ukraine is running critically low on Patriot missiles and other anti-ballistic air defense systems, according to reports. The appeal comes as Russia intensifies mass missile and drone attacks against Ukraine and threatens new strikes on Kyiv. Ukraine’s Presidential Office later confirmed the existence of the letter.

Ukraine Bets on Deep Strikes With ‘Logistical Lockdown’ to Drain Russian Offensive Power — Ukraine’s Ministry of Defense, together with the General Staff, has launched a new program called “Logistical Lockdown” to expand deep strikes on Russian rear infrastructure and logistics networks. Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov said the initiative aims to systematically degrade Russian operational capacity and reduce frontline assaults. Kyiv also reports rising Russian losses per kilometer of advance,

Brussels Finally Has a Timetable for Ukraine’s EU Bid — The European Commission is set to propose opening the first cluster of EU membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova on June 16, with EU leaders expected to approve the move days later. Progress had long been blocked by Hungary’s Viktor Orbán, but his defeat in March’s election has cleared the way. New Prime Minister Péter Magyar has signaled a less obstructive stance, though he is tying cooperation to demands for frozen EU funds to b

‘You No Longer Belong to Yourself Here’ — A woman who once planned her future abroad returned to Ukraine and joined the army. Today, as an evacuation medic, “Bandana” helps keep wounded soldiers alive.

Hungary Links Zelensky Meeting to Progress on Minority Rights — Hungary’s government on Wednesday said a potential meeting between Prime Minister Péter Magyar and President Volodymyr Zelensky will only take place if negotiations over the rights of the Hungarian minority in Ukraine make progress. Technical-level talks between Budapest and Kyiv are already underway as both sides seek to ease diplomatic tensions and improve bilateral relations.

EXPLAINER: So Where Does Ukraine Get So Many ‘Ace’ Drone Pilots From? — Ukraine’s “drone advantage” isn’t just airframes, it’s pilot training. Ukraine runs a massive drone program with ~80,000 personnel involved and an estimated 25-40,000 active combat UAV pilots (elite SBS: ~15k) – outnumbering many nations’ entire air forces. Training grew from 2,014 hobbyists to a nationwide network of military, private, and unit-run schools, turning novices into competent pilots in about one to two months. H

Russian Drone Strike Injures 11 in Odesa Region — Russian forces launched a drone attack against civilian infrastructure in Ukraine’s Odesa region late Wednesday, injuring 11 people, including two children, according to regional authorities. Residential buildings, a Nova Poshta branch and a grocery store were damaged in the strike. Three victims remain in serious condition as emergency crews continue clearing the aftermath of the attack.

Ukraine Launches First War-Risk Insurance Payouts as 4 Kyiv Firms Cut Premium Costs by Two-Thirds — Ukraine’s Export Credit Agency has issued its first payouts under a state war-risk insurance subsidy program, with four Kyiv businesses sharing Hr. 6.8 million ($153,500) in compensation. The program, established under Cabinet Resolution No. 1541, reduces effective insurance costs from 4.24% to 1.19%. The ECA has received 40 applications from companies across multiple regions, covering assets wort

Ukraine Names Bosnian-Serb Fighter a Suspect in War Crime Probe — Ukraine said Davor Savičić, a Bosnian-Serb mercenary fighting for Moscow, has been named a suspect for reportedly torturing civilians north of Kyiv in 2022. Other reports say Savičić previously fought for Serbian paramilitary groups in the Yugoslav civil war and later helped Russia recruit other mercenaries.

‘Old Leak’: Zelensky Adviser Rejects The Economist Claim Ukraine Preparing for ‘Years More’ of War — The Economist on Wednesday reported that Ukraine is preparing for a prolonged war, potentially two to three more years, citing strong battlefield performance and drone warfare success. However, it warns of rising internal political tensions and corruption risks. Zelensky adviser Dmytro Lytvyn rejected the claim as an “old leak.” Peace talks remain uncertain.

Hungary Lawmakers Vote to Stop Orban-Initiated ICC Exit — Hungary’s parliament voted to halt the country’s withdrawal from the International Criminal Court, reversing a move launched by former Prime Minister Viktor Orbán. Pro-EU leader Peter Magyar pushed the repeal through parliament ahead of the June 2 deadline, signaling renewed support for the Hague-based tribunal.

Attack on Kyiv: What are Putin’s Objectives? — Russia’s latest missile and drone strikes on Kyiv, alongside warnings to foreign diplomats, are widely seen by commentators as signs of Kremlin weakness rather than strength. Analysts argue the attacks reflect Putin’s nervousness, fading domestic support, and failed intimidation tactics, while exposing Europe’s slow response and Russia’s attempts to test advanced weapons and pressure Donald Trump.

IMF Mission Arrives in Kyiv as Key Reforms Stall — An IMF mission led by Gavin Gray arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday to begin the first review of Ukraine’s $8.1 billion Extended Fund Facility program, with a $685.5 million tranche expected in early June. Reform progress is mixed: parliament approved the military levy extension, but failed to pass VAT on small parcels, while a proposal on VAT for sole proprietors remains under discussion.

‘Next 6 Months Are the Most Critical’: Ukrainian Commander Sees War ‘Turning Point’ Ahead — Ukrainian Brig. Gen. Andriy Biletsky told Reuters that Russia’s military is becoming exhausted and may soon lose the battlefield initiative. He said the next six months will be critical for Ukraine to improve frontline positions, intensify drone warfare and enter future negotiations with Moscow from a position of strength. Analysts said Russian forces are increasingly strained despite continued pressure i

Ukraine’s BlueBird Tech Joins Pentagon Drone Push as US Eyes Mass UAV Warfare — Ukraine’s BlueBird Tech has joined the Pentagon’s Drone Dominance Program alongside US partner Zaruba, marking deeper integration into US defense innovation. The initiative tests advanced UAV and AI battlefield systems.

Ukraine's Top Mobile Operator Bets $81M on Sun to Cut Grid Dependence — Kyivstar has acquired six solar power plants in the Lviv region for Hr.3.6 billion ($80.8 million), lifting its total renewable energy portfolio to 118 MW, enough to cover roughly 30% of the company’s annual electricity consumption. The deal, cleared by Ukraine’s Antimonopoly Committee in March 2026, is the latest step in a broader energy and digital expansion strategy the Nasdaq-listed telecoms operator has been executing s

Belarusian Democratic Forces Open Mission in Kyiv — On Tuesday, Belarusian democratic forces officially opened a mission in Kyiv aimed at strengthening cooperation with Ukraine and supporting Belarusians living in the country. The new office will coordinate work with Ukrainian institutions, support volunteers and deepen political dialogue between Ukraine and Belarusian opposition groups amid threats of invasion from Belarus under the Lukashenko administration.

Europe Warns Russia Could Escalate War Beyond Ukraine — European security officials on Tuesday reported concerns about Russia potentially escalating the war beyond Ukraine to justify another mobilization wave and pressure NATO. The fears come amid Russian nuclear drills in Belarus, threats toward Baltic states and mounting Russian battlefield losses.

What’s Wrong With Russia? — In a wide-ranging conversation, two prominent Kremlin watchers examine and discuss the effects of Russia’s war against Ukraine on the domestic politics and collective psyche of a nation obsessed with its own greatness. “What was created in Russia was a horrifically degraded regime, which combines the worst qualities of organized crime and communist disregard for human life. It is a danger to the whole world, including in the first order to the Russian people.”

NATO Plans Rapid Troop Deployment For Baltic Defence In Case Of War With Russia — NATO, as of Tuesday, is reportedly preparing plans to rapidly deploy additional corps-level forces to defend the Baltic states in case of conflict with Russia, according to Reuters. The move follows Moscow expanding legal provisions for overseas troop use and renewed rhetoric over the rights of Russians in Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania.

US Denies Visa To Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Ahead Of UN Security Council Session — The US has denied a visa to Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Alexander Alimov, who was due to attend a UN Security Council session, Moscow says. Russia calls the move a breach of obligations and disrespect toward China’s presidency, amid uneven diplomatic contacts between Washington and Moscow.

US to Pull 50% of Deep-Strike Capability from Europe, Sources Tell TVP World — The US plans to cut by half the deep strike forces allocated to Europe, including aircraft carriers and warplanes, shifting more defense responsibility to NATO allies. European countries are expected to present plans by July to fill the gap. The move reflects growing US pressure on Europe to strengthen its own military capabilities amid rising tensions over NATO commitments and deterrence against Russia.

Ukraine Hits Russian Aircraft Plant, Airbase and Oil Refinery in Overnight Barrage — Ukraine launched overnight strikes on Russian military and energy infrastructure, reportedly hitting an aircraft repair plant in Taganrog, the Baltimor airbase in Voronezh, and the Tuapse oil refinery. Occupied Sevastopol also came under missile and drone attack as Moscow claimed to down 140 Ukrainian drones.
ISW Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, May 26, 2026 — Latest from the Institute for the Study of War.

Rubio’s Yerevan Visit: What Washington Said, and What It Left Unspoken — US Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s brief stop in Yerevan signaled support for Armenia’s sovereignty without openly endorsing any candidate before the election. By focusing on concrete agreements, Washington denied Moscow an easy propaganda weapon.

Chernihiv Hit by Massive Russian Attack, at Least 15 Explosions Reported — Chernihiv came under a massive Russian attack in the early hours of Wednesday, with around 15 explosions reported. Local officials said one city enterprise was damaged, while no casualties had been reported.

Norway Contributes €40 Million to Ukraine’s Winter Energy Resilience — Norway will provide €40 million ($46 million) through the EU’s Ukraine Investment Framework to help Ukraine strengthen its energy system before winter, focusing on decentralized power, renewables, battery storage and local resilience amid continued Russian attacks.

Thousands Rally in Georgia for Europe on Independence Day — Thousands of Georgians rallied in Tbilisi on Independence Day, demanding a European future and protesting the government’s democratic backsliding.

More Than 50 Countries Condemn Russia’s Mass Attack on Ukraine at UN — A global coalition of more than 50 countries backed Ukraine at the UN after Russia’s latest mass attack, condemning missile strikes, embassy threats and nuclear-capable weapons.

Ukraine Has 500 Targets Ready in Belarus if Lukashenko Joins War, ‘Magyar’ Says — Ukraine says it has already marked 500 targets in Belarus if Lukashenko enters the war, as tensions rise over alleged drone attacks near the border.

‘Deeply Concerned’ – UN Chief Condemns Russia’s Threats of New Strikes on Kyiv — UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres said he was “deeply concerned” by Russia’s threats to hit Kyiv and its decision-making centers. He said the threat, following one of the heaviest attacks on the Ukrainian capital since the 2022 invasion began, risks further escalation. This, he said, could possibly make any search for peace “even more distant.”
